@colinmegill I’m advocating thinking about the problem more deeply, not ignoring it.

@colinmegill I’m saying that no matter what safeguards you put in, people will find ways to abuse it. And in many cases I think the benefits of good actors using a technology outweighs the benefits of crippling it for bad actors who will creatively abuse any system they come across.

@colinmegill My main critique is focusing on the technology that is used by bad actors instead of the bad actors themselves: they are usually creative and will find means to misuse and abuse, and there are many technologies we shouldn’t dull or sacrifice because of these bad actors.

@colinmegill I’m just saying the world is already pretty free and public-editable and the ways that we deal with those things is different from the technologies at play. 

People have similar complaints about Twitter, Facebook, the internet in general, as if it is some new paradigm.
